meta.title: TechPulse
meta.tagline: Tech & AI news, hot takes, and who's saying what — from the builders you follow
meta.note: Built from your X Following timeline, filtered to tech/AI builders by profile. Click any story to see every take and what different people are saying.

stories.title: The Model Race Turns Into a Trash-Talk Contest
stories.summary: Fable, GPT-5.6 Sol, and Grok 4.5 are all fighting for the same coding-agent throne this week. Sam Altman took direct digs at Anthropic while touting Sol's price and speed, builders are split on which model they actually prefer day-to-day, and Grok 4.5 quietly grabbed the #1 spot on a long-horizon agent benchmark.

takes.name: Elon Musk
takes.text: Grok 4.5 is worth trying

takes.name: dex
takes.text: okay i may have spoke too soon sol cooked on this ui i think the prompt was like "make it more based" https://t.co/4c4Sp5IZF1

takes.name: Kevin Kern
takes.text: a reusable skill built around gpt-5.6 prompting guidance & best practices.

takes.name: Sam Altman
takes.text:
5.6 sol growth is insane.

the inference team has done heroic work to be able to support demand.

we are going to move mountains to continue to scale, but it is possible there are some hiccups soon.

takes.name: WebDevCody
takes.text: Just crossed 1 billion total Grok 4.5 tokens using @cursor_ai https://t.co/Q4RUS8gqVp

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text:
I've used GPT-5.6 Sol heavily for a while now…

And it brought me to a conclusion I didn't expect:

The original Fable 5 is still the best model I have ever used. 

Nothing since has topped it.

Let me be specific about what I mean.

When Fable 5 first launched in June, we had https://t.co/QhXD4USbE7

takes.name: Sam Altman
takes.text:
GPT-5.6 sol is half the price and ~twice as token efficient as fable in many cases for accomplishing the same task.

happy to deliver at one-quarter of the price.

takes.name: Kevin Kern
takes.text:
Using gpt sol med/high for every task drains usage limits pretty quickly and can be unnecessarily slow, so I put together a model cheat sheet.

love to hear your feedback. https://t.co/Qb7OG73JMp

takes.name: Sahaj
takes.text:
I don't know what to use between fable 5 and 5.6 sol

my usual strategy of "use one until it does something dumb, then switch" doesn't work anymore because they're both good enough 😅

although I've found that 5.6 sol is amazing at deleting slop unlike any other model I've tried

takes.name: Jiayuan (JY) Zhang
takes.text: @sama We rewrite the whole product with 5.6 Sol; it's a very solid model. Thanks, Sam!

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Higgsfield MCP/Plugin for After Effects just got upgraded.
Powered by Fable 5.

1. Build your own AE plugins
2. Drop in your reference and it decomposes into editable vector layers you can animate
3. AI assistant that answers or does it for you
4. Localize a project seamlessly https://t.co/WL4pqMgLEM

takes.name: 图拉鼎
takes.text: 用 Grok 4.5 写代码，在我没有特别指示的情况下，它直接给我用上 macOS 27 中新增的 API 了…这个 SDK 还在 Beta 呢，这训练数据也太新了，简直可以称做实时。Grok 的含金量又增加了。 https://t.co/cQKHJuCxEq

takes.name: Abraham John 🦄🦓
takes.text:
Designers, here’s how I created this beautiful hero cursor-following gradient effect with Framer's AI Agent.

→ Open @framer
→ Switch to Agent and choose your Fable 5.
→ Select the hero background and paste the prompt below.
→ Generate the cursor-following gradient effect https://t.co/9gpnA4jmEy

takes.name: Sahaj
takes.text:
why not use subagents?

subagents are inherently complex and hard to observe

/trio is not for long-running tasks, it's when you want fable-level capability without waiting and hour for it to edit files

long tasks often end up being slop so I like to be active in the loop

takes.name: Elon Musk
takes.text: Grok 4.5 reaches #1 position on Long-Horizon Terminal-Bench

takes.name: Jiayuan (JY) Zhang
takes.text:
大家一定要体验一下最新版本的 @MulticaAI，对 UIUX 做了大量的优化，整套 UI 都是 5.6 Sol + Fabel 5 写的，但是几乎没有什么 AI Slop（除了一些个别页面还没有优化完）。

过段时间可以分享一下如何结合这两个模型来做 UI 设计。 https://t.co/igruZiRhJk

takes.name: Ben Davis
takes.text: I still prefer GPT-5.6 btw

takes.name: Micky
takes.text: Fable > 5.6 Sol

takes.name: James Perkins
takes.text: Fable still on top for me

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: I still prefer Fable btw

takes.name: Alem Tuzlak 🇧🇦
takes.text:
@sama The only thing that bugs me about the new models is the fact that they are very slow. I've been running sol on fast mode qnd it took it 4 hours to review and write an implementation doc. 

I stopped it and told it to wrap it up and spend the remainder of my day using it to

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text: I can only imagine what GPT 5.6 Sol Ultra is like...

takes.name: Browserbase
Riley Brown
takes.text:
Niche personal tool that I built with 5.6 Sol this morning. 

An exact clone of Excaladraw but it lets me create animated presentations that i can use in my videos...

And i love adding logos to my excaladraw so i added built in image search and background removal. https://t.co/d0fXaVGm32

takes.name: Mirko Monti
takes.text:
So, Grok 4.5 is a 1.5 T model, Grok 5 would be 10T model... then Grok 6 will be a 100T models? 

Is there a limit?

takes.name: Alem Tuzlak 🇧🇦
takes.text:
What really had me scratching my head was why did they call it Fable 5. It was a new line of models, why not just Fable?

Would make a hell of a lot more sense and make it easier to name things

takes.name: Sam Altman
takes.text: come for the best model, stay because we don’t treat you with contempt

takes.name: Sam Altman
takes.text: still sorta breaks my brain to see our models be good at design finally

takes.name: Sam Altman
takes.text: i thought this was satire, kept looking for the handle to be spelled c1audeai or something

takes.name: Sam Altman
takes.text: "hard questions are great but only if we deem you worthy enough to not silently downgrade you, or even get access at all"

takes.name: Sam Altman
takes.text:
2.5x increase in usage of our agentic products (codex and chatgpt work) in the last week!

welcome.

takes.name: Sam Altman
takes.text: this would have been a whole startup not too long ago

takes.name: Sam Altman
takes.text: also, a reason to favor open-source harnesses.

stories.title: Agent Skills Keep Compounding Into a Real Ecosystem
stories.summary: The 'skills' pattern is maturing fast: Matt Pocock is shipping new Claude skills and a live demo of /wayfinder, Emil Kowalski's skills repo just crossed 8,000 GitHub stars, and GitHub Copilot CLI shipped smarter subagent delegation that cuts tool failures by up to 27%.

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
Sometimes you don't know the answer in a grilling session

Thinking about creating a /to-questionnaire skill

That takes the grilling session and turns it into a questionnaire you can send someone to fill out OR work through it together in a meeting

Collaboration made easy

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
Let's do a quick check on how wayfinder is going:

When you build something new with my skills, you reach for:

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
Yesterday I submitted a Claude plugin for my skills (finally)

Anyone know how long the submission process takes? Would love a nudge from any of my mates at Anthropic

takes.name: Emil Kowalski
takes.text:
My skills repo has gained 8,000 GitHub stars in a week, it surpassed Vaul and is 300 stars short of surpassing Sonner. 

Not greatest metrics of success, but it’s a good indication of how the times change. https://t.co/tpdjhy92Ly

takes.name: joel ⛈️
takes.text:
a 2-hour edit of a workshop called "loopcraft in pi" where i explore pi as a prototyping tool for durable autonomous agent loops (in a sandbox) with effect and xstate as low level guard rails 

https://t.co/TIkX6CHnOp

takes.name: joel ⛈️
takes.text: working on some memory system experiments for @joelclaw distilling agent sessions, email, slack, x, etc into an observation graph that can be organized into various projects (this is a "newspaper" page, also has a voice mode) https://t.co/G1rYp5FigP

takes.name: Google DeepMind
takes.text: Here’s how we used the Predicting the Past Skill in Google @Antigravity to track down a Roman ring thief, map an ancient cult across Europe, and reconstruct the networks of people visiting a Greek oracle. 🧵 https://t.co/Qz8lXnxbkJ

takes.name: Google DeepMind
takes.text:
🔮 Who visited the Oracle of Dodona?

By analyzing collections of ancient lead tablets, the Skill mapped visitors traveling from across the ancient world. It reconstructed the community of oracle visitors, turning scattered fragments into a connected network. https://t.co/1NKaUzzYPA

takes.name: Google DeepMind
takes.text:
These examples showcase how the Predicting the Past Skill can push forward historical research using advanced AI models and complex workflows - with no coding required.

Find out more → https://t.co/ItIg8BFKgu

takes.name: GitHub
takes.text:
Get faster, more reliable coding sessions in GitHub Copilot CLI.

We implemented smarter subagent delegation that cut tool failures 23%, search failures 27%, and edit failures 18%, with no quality regression. https://t.co/iLxGRZwmJT

stories.title: "Just Show Me What You Built" - Devs Push Back on Agent-Setup Flexing
stories.summary: A backlash is brewing against posts bragging about elaborate custom agent setups instead of shipped work. thdxr called it out directly, webdevcody calls it 'the great humbling,' and ThePrimeagen admits he actually misses the simple joy of hand-programming.

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
please i'm begging you show me something you built

not another "this is my custom agent setup" post where you pretend you're doing something smarter than vanilla claude code

please

takes.name: teej dv 🔭
takes.text: @thdxr Even better, I'm building a programming language

takes.name: WebDevCody
takes.text:
We live in the era of the great humbling, where skills you spent years learning can be outperformed by a single prompt.

You may not be as important as you thought you were.

Now go build your self-worth around something technology can’t easily commoditize.

takes.name: trash
takes.text: another week at the prompt factory https://t.co/bmKjabHVkC

takes.name: ThePrimeagen
takes.text:
This actually makes me sad.  Programming is such a fun thing to do in of itself.  The completion of the thing task is the ultimate goal, but the road to get there is enjoyable!

Even as i investigate loops and think deeply about how to deploy AI in a real manner that produces

stories.title: Coding Agents Get Real Browsers and Ditch the Desktop App
stories.summary: Codex and Claude Code both shipped in-app browser upgrades (tabs, cookie/password import) within two days of each other, Browserbase's Julius integration shows what agents-with-browsers can actually do, and even T3 Code's own creator says he barely opens the desktop app anymore.

takes.name: Riley Brown
takes.text:
Codex just updated their in app browser... You can now open multiple tabs, import cookies and passwords and more. 

And then 2 days later Claude Code released a similar update...

This completely shifts the way we use computers...
Here's everything you need to know.

00:00 Intro https://t.co/5SJx7aAtsC

takes.name: Paul Klein IV
takes.text:
every great agent needs a browser. 

we’re excited to continue to power great companies building great agents.

congrats to the Julius team on the big launch!

takes.name: Browserbase
Ben Davis
takes.text:
Realized over the last month that for coding agent "apps" (like codex desktop app)

Web app > real desktop app

I use the t3 code web app constantly, I never touch the desktop app

1) It's more convenient to just open it in the browser, I'm always in there anyway

2) I can host

stories.title: Vercel Keeps Shipping Agent-Native Infrastructure
stories.summary: Vercel's push into 'infrastructure for agents' continues: AgentMail landed on the Vercel Marketplace for agent email, a new free Academy course teaches durable agent deployment, and feature-flag tooling now lets agents self-tune experiments.

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: The @agentmail team has been cooking. Tell your agent to 𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕 𝚒𝚗𝚜𝚝𝚊𝚕𝚕 𝚊𝚐𝚎𝚗𝚝𝚖𝚊𝚒𝚕 – no signup, automatic setup and unified billing.

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
AgentMail is on the Vercel Marketplace.

Your agents can send, receive, and reply to email from a real inbox.
• Full thread memory
• Structured data extraction
• Deliverability handled

Run 𝚟𝚌 𝚒 𝚊𝚐𝚎𝚗𝚝𝚖𝚊𝚒𝚕 or learn more ↓
https://t.co/GB3UQ0CxAu

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
We launched a free Vercel Academy course on building @evedev_ agents.

Learn how to ship a durable agent to production: set up a project, call tools, add a human-in-the-loop, and deliver the agent through @SlackHQ. https://t.co/FGSSupAizv

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
Agent Runs now shows subagent activity for @evedev_ projects.

Inspect each run in full, including turns, model and tool calls, cost, and token usage.

https://t.co/sha40IgCrT

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text:
Powerful building block for autonomous, self-optimizing websites and applications.

Give agents the ability to set up and tune experiments with feature flags.

stories.title: New Benchmarks Try to Capture What Agents Actually Do
stories.summary: Dex Horthy rounded up the latest SOTA coding-agent benchmark results, while Francois Chollet highlighted Morpheus, a new benchmark designed to capture non-stationary, real-world agent deployment rather than static episodic tasks.

takes.name: dex
takes.text: SOTA Coding Agent Benchmarks: 🦄 AI That Works #65 https://t.co/zHsdSn1Hc5

takes.name: François Chollet
takes.text:
Super impressed with what Harvinder and Suman have built at @airtap_ai. They've essentially turned SMS into a headless agentic execution layer for your mobile apps.

You just text it to run errands, and it operates apps like DoorDash, TikTok, etc. in the background. It keeps you

stories.title: Theo's T3 Code Keeps Evolving Into a Full Cloud Dev Platform
stories.summary: Theo is iterating fast on T3 Code: a new experimental sidebar UX, a deep dive on Codex's misunderstood 'Ultra' mode, and admitting he barely opens the desktop app anymore since the web version can control all his dev boxes.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
It's still so, so cool that I can control all of my dev boxes through T3 Code. I barely open the desktop app anymore, I just use the site and the app.

Working on 3 projects with 2 harnesses across 4 dev machines, all from one (open source) interface 🫡 https://t.co/fveG2WyfQ2

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: I want to try a new experimental sidebar for threads in T3 Code. Generated a few options. Which do you like best? https://t.co/UIPXHS6dFt

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: TIL most people didn't know that Codex has a question tool (because they gate it to plan mode for some reason??)

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: "Ultra" mode is not what you think. I did a deep dive on Codex's newest feature to try and help y'all understand it better. https://t.co/bAqLBDLZVq

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: @davis7 Performance isn’t where it should be on T3 Code web (I have spent the last 2 days fixing it)

stories.title: Elon Musk's SpaceXAI Data-Retention Flip-Flop
stories.summary: Musk first defended retaining some user data for debugging purposes, then hours later reversed course entirely, promising all data uploaded to SpaceXAI before now will be "completely and utterly deleted." Both posts pulled huge engagement.

takes.name: Elon Musk
takes.text:
True. 

As a precautionary measure, all user data that was uploaded to SpaceXAI before now will be completely and utterly deleted. Zero anything whatsoever will remain.

takes.name: Elon Musk
takes.text:
SpaceX policy regarding data retention.

It is actually helpful for debugging issues if we can retain some amount of data, so allowing this would be appreciated, but your privacy settings are always respected.

stories.title: Claude for Teachers Brings Free AI to K-12 Classrooms
stories.summary: Anthropic launched free access to premium Claude for verified K-12 educators in the US, complete with a teaching-skills library mapped to academic standards in all 50 states — one of the highest-engagement single tweets of the window.

takes.name: Claude
takes.text:
We're introducing Claude for Teachers: free access to premium Claude capabilities for verified K-12 educators in the US, with a library of teaching skills and a direct connection to evidence-based curricula, mapped to academic standards in all 50 states.

https://t.co/5hZZijVPCV https://t.co/5ofG8YLEON

stories.title: Fei-Fei Li Pushes on Long-Horizon Robotics Planning
stories.summary: Fei-Fei Li argues that long-horizon, complex everyday tasks remain unsolved for today's robotics, requiring planning and object-detection capabilities well beyond current systems.

takes.name: Fei-Fei Li
takes.text:
AI’s next chapter will be defined not only by technical progress, but by how responsibly and thoughtfully we bring it into the world.

I’m looking forward to be speaking on stage at Ai4 2026, Aug 4–6 in Vegas. Register here at https://t.co/iAWUZf994t

#Ai42026

stories.title: Epic Games Store Hits 78 Million Monthly Users
stories.summary: Tim Sweeney marked the Epic Games Store's growth to 78 million monthly active users since its 2018 launch, crediting outgoing leadership as new leadership takes over.

takes.name: Tim Sweeney
takes.text:
Thanks to Epic veteran Steve Allison for launching the Epic Games Store in 2018 and driving it to 78 million monthly active users today!

Epic Games Store has new leadership lined up and we'll be making an announcement on July 27 ahead of big plans for 2026 and 2027.

hotTakes.name: shadcn
hotTakes.text: I like how GPTs talk to me. OpenAI got this right.

hotTakes.name: Figma
hotTakes.text: If you use a trackpad who hurt you

hotTakes.name: Matt Pocock
hotTakes.text:
Never been easier to feel busy and ship nothing

Stay focused

hotTakes.name: Pirate Software
hotTakes.text:
The Twitch AI Summary is still listening to your broadcast and forming summaries even when disabled. 

I have had this feature turned off since it was implemented and decided to test it live. I spoke about how it likely is still listening in and then turned it on and didn't speak https://t.co/btPsFQMNgy

hotTakes.name: Garry Tan
hotTakes.text:
Your tax dollars at work

The doom loop in SF isn’t just about ruining the city for normal citizens

It’s also about looting the public good

hotTakes.name: Ryo Lu
hotTakes.text:
when the dream becomes the job

i used to think the dream was to make my hobby my work.

to spend my life close to the things i loved: computers, tools, interfaces, systems, tiny details, the feeling of making something from nothing. the things i used to do at night, after

hotTakes.name: dax
hotTakes.text:
the opensource option tends to be shittier but you use it because it's free

we're trying to make the opensource option also the high quality option
